categories.name: Calculus
categories.description: Required calculus sequence for all EE students.

categories.name: Multivariate/Vector Calculus
categories.description: Choose one multivariate/vector calculus sequence. AM 30 is Multivariate Calculus for Engineers; AM 100 is Mathematical Methods for Engineers. MATH 23A/23B is Vector Calculus I and II.

categories.name: Linear Algebra & Differential Equations
categories.description: Choose one linear algebra and differential equations sequence. AM 10/20 preferred for EE majors.

categories.name: Probability & Statistics
categories.description: Choose one probability/statistics course. Note: CSE 16 is NOT a major requirement for EE.

categories.name: Physics
categories.description: Required physics sequence. PHYS 15A may substitute for PHYS 5A/L; PHYS 15C may substitute for PHYS 5C/N (honors alternatives; consult BE advising).

categories.name: Lower Division Engineering & Programming
categories.description: Required lower-division courses. CSE 20 is Beginning Programming in Python (required prereq for ECE 13). CSE 12 is Computer Systems and Assembly Language. ECE 13 is Computer Systems and C Programming. ECE 80T is waived for transfer students. CSE 30 is recommended but not required.

categories.name: Upper Division EE Core
categories.description: Required upper-division electrical engineering and logic design courses.

categories.name: Design Elective
categories.description: One of the four concentration electives must be a design elective, chosen from this list. Must be taken before ECE 129A.

categories.name: Concentration Electives
categories.description: Choose 3 additional concentration elective courses (minimum 3 from one track). Tracks: Electronics/Optics (ECE 104, 115, 118, 121, 130/L, 136, 141, 145, 149, 157/L, 163, 167, 169, 172, 173, 175/L, 176/L, 177/L, 178, 180J, 183, 185) or Communications/Signals/Systems (ECE 118, 130/L, 136, 141, 145, 149, 152, 153, 163, 169, 183). Graduate-level courses may be used with department approval.

categories.name: Comprehensive Requirement (Capstone)
categories.description: Complete capstone requirement: either ECE 129A/B/C sequence, or ECE 129A plus ECE 195 (Senior Thesis, 10 units, requires department approval). Prerequisites: ECE 171/L, CSE 100/L, and at least one design elective.
